concept: Feral hermit and accidental witness to a buried secret
appearance:
Kerry moves through the desert like a ghost who's forgotten how to be human, his presence so attuned to the rhythms of the wasteland that he seems more animal than Kindred. As a Gangrel hermit who's survived decades in the desert, he presents as a man who might have been in his thirties when Embraced, but years of isolation and paranoia have left him looking older, more weathered, as if the desert itself has carved its mark into his flesh. He's lean and wiry, with the kind of build that speaks to years of hard living: muscles hardened by constant movement, skin tanned and leathery from exposure, and a posture that suggests someone who's learned to move with the economy of a predator. His face carries the sharp, angular features of someone who's spent years squinting into sun and wind, and his eyes—constantly scanning, constantly assessing—hold the kind of intensity that comes from learning to see threats before they materialize.

His clothing is a study in functional survival: tattered pieces that have seen more miles than most people drive in a lifetime, a backpack that's been repaired more times than it's been replaced, and practical items chosen for their ability to withstand desert conditions. Everything is worn and weathered, chosen for function over form, and he has a habit of carrying animal totems—small carvings or bones that speak to his connection with the predators that call the wasteland home. His hair is kept practical and unkempt, as if he's learned that in the desert, survival matters more than presentation, and his grooming is minimal, as if he's forgotten that such things matter.

What makes Kerry truly unsettling, however, isn't just his appearance but the way he carries himself despite the knowledge of what he's witnessed. He's half-mad from isolation but retains flashes of insight, someone who witnessed the 1973 Prince's staking and guards that secret with paranoia and fear. He refers to the staked Prince as 'The Still One,' and sometimes speaks as though Elijah is nearby, as if the memory of what he saw has become a ghost that follows him through the desert. His paranoia and claustrophobia define his behavior, and he constantly suspects others of manipulation, resisting trust-building unless shown compassion. His Animalism and Protean are sharp from years of survival, and his acute senses allow him to detect Kindred scent and vibrations through soil or wind. In the shadowed trails of the desert or the quiet corners of Phoenix's Kindred society, Kerry is a reminder that sometimes the most dangerous secrets are the ones that drive you mad, and that in the world of the Gangrel, survival sometimes means becoming something that no longer looks human.
biography: A wandering Gangrel who has survived decades in the desert. Kerry witnessed the 1973 Prince’s staking and guards that secret with paranoia and fear.
notes: Kerry is half-mad from isolation but retains flashes of insight. He refers to the staked Prince as 'The Still One.' His paranoia and claustrophobia define his behavior.
equipment: Tattered backpack, desert survival tools, knife, animal totems.

merits_flaws.name: Acute Senses (Hearing and Smell)
merits_flaws.description: Kerry’s animalistic senses allow him to detect Kindred scent and vibrations through soil or wind.

merits_flaws.name: Claustrophobia
merits_flaws.description: Must test to remain calm in enclosed spaces or underground; failure triggers panic or frenzy.

merits_flaws.name: Haunted Memories
merits_flaws.description: Hears whispers from the staked Prince in dreams; sometimes speaks as though Elijah is nearby.

merits_flaws.name: Paranoia
merits_flaws.description: Constantly suspects others of manipulation; resists trust-building unless shown compassion.
